name: deeptools
description: NGS analysis toolkit. BAM to bigWig conversion, QC (correlation, PCA, fingerprints), heatmaps/profiles (TSS, peaks), for ChIP-seq, RNA-seq, ATAC-seq visualization.
capabilities: Convert BAM alignment files to normalized bigWig coverage tracks
Generate quality control metrics including correlation, PCA, and fingerprint plots
Create heatmaps and profile plots around genomic features like TSS and peaks
Compare treatment versus control samples with log2 ratio tracks
Validate input files for proper indexing and format
Generate ready-to-run workflow scripts for common analyses
author: K-Dense-AI
securitySummary: All 519 static findings are FALSE_POSITIVES. The scanner misinterpreted markdown documentation examples with backticks as shell execution, 'SAM files' (Sequence Alignment/Map format) as Windows SAM database, and mentions of bioinformatics tools (samtools, plotFingerprint) as security threats. The Python scripts perform legitimate workflow generation for NGS analysis. No actual security risks present.
valueStatement: Process and visualize next-generation sequencing data. Convert BAM to bigWig, generate quality control plots, and create publication-ready heatmaps for ChIP-seq, RNA-seq, and ATAC-seq experiments.
limitations: Requires BAM files to be properly indexed with .bai files
Does not perform peak calling or read alignment
Large genome files require significant memory and processing time
Results depend on correct normalization method selection

useCases.title: QC for ChIP-seq experiments
useCases.description: Validate ChIP quality, assess enrichment strength, and compare replicates before downstream analysis.

useCases.title: Coverage track generation
useCases.description: Create normalized bigWig files from BAM alignments for genome browser visualization.

useCases.title: Sample comparison
useCases.description: Compare multiple samples with correlation analysis and generate publication-ready heatmaps.

promptTemplates.title: Basic conversion
promptTemplates.prompt: Convert my sample.bam file to a normalized bigWig coverage track using RPGC normalization for hg38 genome.

promptTemplates.title: Quality check
promptTemplates.prompt: Check the quality of my ChIP-seq experiment by generating a fingerprint plot and correlation heatmap.

promptTemplates.title: Visualization
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a heatmap showing ChIP signal around transcription start sites for my H3K4me3 data.

promptTemplates.title: Full workflow
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a complete ChIP-seq analysis workflow from BAM files to heatmaps comparing treatment versus input control.

bestPractices: Always validate BAM files with the validation script before analysis to ensure indices exist
Choose appropriate normalization method: RPGC for ChIP-seq, CPM for RNA-seq bins, RPKM for gene-level analysis
Use --ignoreDuplicates to remove PCR duplicates unless specifically analyzing clonality
antiPatterns: Do not use --extendReads for RNA-seq analysis as it would extend across splice junctions
Avoid mixing normalization methods when comparing multiple samples
Do not skip quality control steps before detailed analysis

faq.question: What normalization method should I use for ChIP-seq?
faq.answer: Use RPGC for ChIP-seq coverage tracks or CPM for simple normalization. Both require effective genome size.

faq.question: Why is my BAM file giving an error?
faq.answer: Ensure the BAM file has a corresponding .bai index file. Run: samtools index yourfile.bam

faq.question: Can I use this for ATAC-seq data?
faq.answer: Yes. Use alignmentSieve with --ATACshift to apply Tn5 correction before coverage generation.

faq.question: What genome size should I use?
faq.answer: Human hg38: 2913022398, Mouse mm10: 2652783500, Fly dm6: 142573017. Use the effective genome size, not assembly size.

faq.question: How do I compare treatment vs control?
faq.answer: Use bamCompare with --operation log2 to create a log2 ratio track of ChIP over input.

faq.question: Why are my heatmaps empty?
faq.answer: Check that your bigWig and BED files use matching genome assemblies and chromosomes.
